```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position - making the 'Yourself a archeologist' part optional
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\n(?:Yourself a archeologist\n)?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            # The map ends before the "Agent the Digger" line or similar stats line.
            # Find the first line that looks like agent stats and chop off the rest.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find the actual start and end lines of the map data itself, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Renamed from closed_doors
                    fountains = []    
                    stairs_up = []    
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Explicitly identify '}' as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\n(?:cursor:|map:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        if message_content:
            # Try to parse multiple inventory items
            inventory_matches = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\n|$)", message_content)
            
            # If inventory_matches is empty, try to parse a single inventory item or a general message
            if inventory_matches:
                for key, item in inventory_matches:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip().replace('.', '')}.") # Remove trailing dot from item description
            elif not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content):
                # Only add as a generic game message if it's not a known action prompt/feedback
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    # Made "Yourself a archeologist" optional and added optional whitespace
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    closed_doors = [] # Changed from `closed_doors` to `door_like_symbols`
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Label as door-like symbols to avoid premature classification
                                closed_doors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F':
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<':
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if closed_doors: # Report as Door-like symbols now
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closed doors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(closed_doors)))}.")
                    if fountains:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\n(?=cursor:|map:)|$)", observation_text)
    # The previous regex `(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))` might miss cases if there is no `\n\n`
    # before cursor/map. The updated one `(?=\n\n(?=cursor:|map:)|$)` should be more robust
    # by explicitly looking for `\n\n` before `cursor` or `map`.
    
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        # Try to find inventory items first
        # Updated regex to handle 'j - a horn.  k - a pine wand.' correctly.
        # It looks for `key - description` followed by `.` and then `  ` or the end of the string.
        # This will get each item individually even if on the same line.
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Find all occurrences of "([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.  |$)"
        # The issue with the previous re was that `.*?` was too greedy, and the lookahead was not correctly bounded.
        # Let's try to match items such that their description doesn't wrongly span to the next item
        item_pattern = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(\. {2}|$|\.$)") # This regex has issues if "pine wand." is `.` in description
        
        # Simpler approach: find `key - description` pairs and refine description later
        all_matches = re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=(?:[.]{2,}|[a-z] - |\s*$))", message_content)
        # ^ This attempts to stop `.*?` at multiple dots, or next item, or end of string.
        
        # For simplicity and robustness to actual format, let's process lines for items
        # If inventory items are always on one line of "key - item_description." format
        
        # Revise inventory regex (original simpler was almost correct but for the greediness)
        # re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\. {2}|[a-z] - |\.$)", message_content)
        # The problem was `.*?` and `\.  ` failing on single space
        
        # Let's use the explicit example: j - a horn.  k - a pine wand.
        # Target: `('j', 'a horn')`, `('k', 'a pine wand')`
        # Need to capture the entire phrase and then remove the dot IF it's not part of the item name
        # And ensure separation between "a horn." and "k -".
        
        # Using a pattern that captures the item description and the trailing dot (if present)
        # and then processing it. The key is how to delineate between multiple items on one line.
        
        # Let's split by the pattern that separates distinct items
        item_separator_pattern = r'\. {2,}(?=[a-z] -)' # Matches ".  " and looks ahead for next item
        parts = re.split(item_separator_pattern, message_content)
        
        # Keep track if any items were found this way
        found_any_items = False
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip()
            if not part:
                continue
            
            # Match an item: key - description.
            match = re.search(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.$|$)", part)
            if match:
                key = match.group(1)
                description = match.group(2).strip()
                # Remove a trailing dot if it's not part of the description (e.g., "wand.")
                if description.endswith('.'):
                    # Check if the dot is truly a separator or part of name. Given the example "pine wand."
                    # it means dot is part of the original object description.
                    pass # Don't strip '.' if it's the observed form.
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {description}.")
                found_any_items = True
            elif not found_any_items and message_content.strip() == part.strip():
                # If no inventory items were found by splitting, and this one part is the whole message
                # Handle as a general game message, but avoid cursor/map content
                if not re.match(r"^\s*$", part) and not re.match(r"^[Cc]ursor:|^[Mm]ap:", part):
                    # Only add if not an empty message and not a misidentified cursor/map section
                    extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {part}.")
        
        # Fallback for "In what direction?" style messages, which don't fit the 'key - item' format
        # and might not be splittable meaningfully.
        if not found_any_items and not extracted_info and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content):
            # Ensure it's not map/cursor lines accidentally included which the message_match should prevent
            # but as a safeguard
            if not re.match(r"^[Cc]ursor:[\s\S]*|^[Mm]ap:[\s\S]*", message_content):
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content.strip()}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    # The 'Yourself a archeologist' line may be missing, and there can be varying whitespace.
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist\s*)?\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    # This block is correctly conditional on agent_x and agent_y being found.
    # The map parsing logic itself appears sound.
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            
            # Filter map lines from everything below agent stats.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line:
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find actual start and end of map, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines.
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip():
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1: # Only proceed if actual map lines were found
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # Find agent's visual position on the map
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    # Calculate offset for absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    closed_doors = [] # Changed symbol interpretation in the output description
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Label as closed doors
                                closed_doors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F':
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<':
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if closed_doors: # Consistent with belief: label as "Closed doors" initially
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closed doors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(closed_doors)))}.")
                    if fountains:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    # This regex ensures message content is precisely delimited.
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\n(?:cursor:|map:|Agent the Digger)|$)", observation_text)
    # The change in lookahead ensures it correctly stops before "cursor:", "map:", or "Agent the Digger" block.
    
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        # New robust regex for inventory items on a single line
        # This regex looks for:
        # ([a-z]) -        -> Capture the item key and " - "
        # (.*?)             -> Non-greedily capture the item description
        # (?=\. {2}[a-z] -  -> Lookahead for ".  " followed by another item (meaning two spaces after dot)
        # |\n[a-z] -        -> OR a newline followed by another item (for multi-line inventory)
        # |$                -> OR the end of the message content
        # The key detail for `j - a horn.  k - a pine wand.` is that descriptions end before `.` then `  `
        # However, `pine wand.` means the `.` is part of description.
        # Let's try to capture 'key - description' and then refine the description if it contains a trailing '.'
        
        # A simple pattern to extract item key and full description, assuming each item starts on a 'key -' line or after 'X.  '
        # This uses non-greedy matching for description and looks for common delimiters for the end of an item.
        inventory_items = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\n[a-z] - |\s*$)", message_content)
        
        if inventory_items:
            for key, item in inventory_items:
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")
        elif message_content.strip(): # If no inventory items were found but there's still message content
            # Ensure it's not a misparsed system section (e.g., cursor or map)
            if not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content) and \
               not re.match(r"^(?:cursor:|map:|Agent the Digger|St:)", message_content, re.MULTILINE):
                # Log the entire residual message content as a general game message
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content.strip()}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:

                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):

                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x

                                closed_doors.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:

        elif message_content.strip(): # If no inventory items were found but there's still message content
            # Ensure it's not a misparsed system section (e.g., cursor or map content)
            # Use re.MULTILINE to check for patterns across multiple lines within 'message_content'
            if not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content) and \
               not re.match(r"^(?:cursor:|map:|Agent the Digger|St:)", message_content, re.MULTILINE):
                # Log the entire residual message content as a general game message
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content.strip()}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")

            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):

            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Renamed from closed_doors based on feedback

                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Explicitly identify '}' as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()

                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(

        observation_text
    )

        extracted_info.append(

        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

- The `---` and `|` symbols represent wall segments. These are not openable doors.

</world_knowledge>
<policy>

- When an interactive object is explicitly identified by the perception module, move towards its *identified coordinates*.
- After reaching an environmental feature identified as a "Closed door" by the perception module, if the 'open' action fails ("You see no door there."), attempt the 'kick' action next.

=== CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Renamed from closed_doors based on feedback
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Explicitly identify '}' as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        for match in re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content):
            inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
        
        if inventory_items_found:
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
=== END CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===

```python
def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
```

```python
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching

    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2

                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:

        extracted_info.append(

        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    # Reverted to "Closed doors" to align with QA feedback for consistency
                    closed_doors = [] 
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Reverted to identify '}' as "Closed doors"
                                closed_doors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if closed_doors: # Report closed doors
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closed doors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(closed_doors)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        for match in re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content):
            inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
        
        if inventory_items_found:
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

=== CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\nYourself a archeologist\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            # The map ends before the "Agent the Digger" line or similar stats line.
            # Find the first line that looks like agent stats and chop off the rest.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find the actual start and end lines of the map data itself, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    closed_doors = [] # New list for closed doors
                    fountains = []    # New list for fountains
                    stairs_up = []    # New list for stairs up
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Explicitly identify '}' as closed doors
                                closed_doors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if closed_doors: # Report closed doors
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closed doors observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(closed_doors)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_items = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  |[a-z] - |\.$)", message_content)
        if inventory_items:
            for key, item in inventory_items:
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")
        # Added a check to ensure we don't try to parse action prompts/feedback as inventory items
        # The previous single_item_match logic was flawed for handling game messages vs inventory.
        # This re.match prevents parsing "In what direction?" or "You see no door there." as inventory.
        elif message_content and not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content):
            single_item_match = re.match(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)\.$", message_content)
            if single_item_match:
                key, item = single_item_match.groups()
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
=== END CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\nYourself a archeologist\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            # The map ends before the "Agent the Digger" line or similar stats line.
            # Find the first line that looks like agent stats and chop off the rest.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find the actual start and end lines of the map data itself, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Changed from closed_doors to door_like_symbols
                    fountains = []    
                    stairs_up = []    
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Label as 'Door-like symbols'
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report as 'Door-like symbols'
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_items = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  |[a-z] - |\.$)", message_content)
        if inventory_items:
            for key, item in inventory_items:
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")
        elif message_content and not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content):
            single_item_match = re.match(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)\.$", message_content)
            if single_item_match:
                key, item = single_item_match.groups()
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Changed from closed_doors to door_like_symbols
                    fountains = []    
                    stairs_up = []    
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Label '}' as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items
    # Refined regex to capture messages more broadly and inventory specifically.
    message_content_block_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_content_block_match:
        message_content = message_content_block_match.group(1).strip()
        
        # Split message_content by newline to handle multiple lines, each potentially an item or a message.
        # This will also help to avoid capturing "In what direction?" as an item.
        message_lines = message_content.split('\n')
        
        for line in message_lines:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue

            # Check if it's an inventory item (e.g., "j - a horn.")
            inventory_item_match = re.match(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.  |$)", line)
            if inventory_item_match:
                key, item = inventory_item_match.groups()
                # Clean up trailing spaces or punctuation from the item string
                item = item.strip().rstrip('.')
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item}.")
            # If it's not an inventory item, and not a known prompt/feedback, it could be a general message.
            # For now, stick to extracting explicit inventory and known map/cursor features.
            # The prompt "In what direction?" should not be extracted as an item.
            # The original logic sometimes interpreted "In what direction?" as "j - n what direction?" accidentally.
            # This refined approach isolates `[a-z] - ...` for inventory.

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    # Made "Yourself a archeologist\n" optional as it may not always be present.
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\n(?:Yourself a archeologist\n)?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            # The map ends before the "Agent the Digger" line or similar stats line.
            # Find the first line that looks like agent stats and chop off the rest.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find the actual start and end lines of the map data itself, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    door_like_symbols = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to reflect uncertainty
                    fountains = []    
                    stairs_up = []    
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Identified as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_items = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  |[a-z] - |\.$)", message_content)
        if inventory_items:
            for key, item in inventory_items:
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")
        # The single_item_match logic was flawed if multiple items are on one line, but was an *elif* to inventory_items
        # So it only ran if inventory_items was empty from findall (which is not the case for multiple items).
        # Removed the single_item_match fallback, as re.findall should handle both cases correctly.
        # Added a check to ensure we don't try to parse action prompts/feedback as inventory items
        # The message_content check `not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content)` is still important if no items are found.
        elif message_content and not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content):
            # If findall found nothing, but there's a message, and it's not a known prompt, could be a single item not ending with '.  ' but just '.'
            # The current findall should handle single items ending with '.', so this elif block can likely be removed.
            # Example: "a - an apple." (should be caught by findall with \.$)
            # Reverting: The original structure was implicitly designed to catch cases where findall might miss,
            # but upon review, findall with the updated lookahead should catch single items too.
            # However, for safety, removed explicit single_item_match, assuming findall is robust.
            # Reintegrating the filtering if no inventory items were found but there's "other" message content.
            pass # No specific action if message contains no inventory items.


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    # Updated regex to handle cases where "Yourself a archeologist" might be missing
    cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\n(?:Yourself a archeologist\n)?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    if cursor_match:
        agent_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_start = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_content_start != -1:
            map_content = observation_text[map_content_start + len("map:\n"):].split('\n')
            # The map ends before the "Agent the Digger" line or similar stats line.
            # Find the first line that looks like agent stats and chop off the rest.
            map_lines = []
            for line in map_content:
                if "Agent the Digger" in line or "St:" in line: # Common patterns for stat lines
                    break
                map_lines.append(line)

            # Find the actual start and end lines of the map data itself, ignoring leading/trailing empty lines
            first_map_line = -1
            last_map_line = -1
            for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if line.strip(): # if line is not empty
                    if first_map_line == -1:
                        first_map_line = i
                    last_map_line = i
            
            if first_map_line != -1:
                # Trim map_lines to only contain the non-empty, relevant map part
                map_lines = map_lines[first_map_line : last_map_line + 1]

                # First, find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x, visual_agent_y = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y = r_idx # r_idx is the y_offset within the trimmed map_lines
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x != -1 and visual_agent_y != -1:
                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    # Renamed to "Door-like symbols" to reflect the uncertain nature as per CURRENT BELIEFS.
                    door_like_symbols = [] 
                    fountains = []    # New list for fountains
                    stairs_up = []    # New list for stairs up
                    
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y

                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Explicitly identify '}' as door-like symbols
                                door_like_symbols.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if door_like_symbols: # Report door-like symbols
                        extracted_info.append(f"Door-like symbols observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(door_like_symbols)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")


    # Extract inventory items
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        # Updated regex to capture multiple inventory items on one line more robustly
        # It looks for "letter - description" followed by a period and then either another "letter - "
        # or the end of the message string.
        inventory_items = re.findall(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)\.(?=\s*[a-z] - |\s*$)", message_content)
        if inventory_items:
            for key, item in inventory_items:
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")
        # Added a check to ensure we don't try to parse action prompts/feedback as inventory items
        # The previous single_item_match logic was flawed for handling game messages vs inventory.
        # This re.match prevents parsing "In what direction?" or "You see no door there." as inventory.
        elif message_content and not re.match(r"^(In what direction\?|You see no door there\.)$", message_content):
            # This part attempts to catch single items that might not fit the regex for multiple items
            # or messages that are single inventory items directly.
            # Updated to ensure it is not an action prompt
            single_item_match = re.match(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)\.$", message_content)
            if single_item_match:
                key, item = single_item_match.groups()
                extracted_info.append(f"Inventory item '{key}': {item.strip()}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```
